description:
## Goal

Realize the refinement records as actual CLIF bodies — but only
when the target wants them. JIT emits canonical only; AOT emits
canonical + every refinement with eligibility=Some. Re-walk
annotations after emission so direct callsites resolve to the
best emitted SpecId.

Split golden CLIF into per-target files: expected.clif.jit and
expected.clif.aot. They diverge meaningfully starting from this
ticket.

## What lands

- New enum in src/ir_codegen.rs:
  ```
  pub enum EmitTarget {
      Jit,        // canonical-only; refinements stay as records
      AotO2,      // canonical + emit every eligible refinement
  }
  ```
  (AotOs and AotO3 are future tickets; not in v1 scope.)

- Codegen entry point takes EmitTarget. Spec selection for
  emission consults the canonical+refinements vec:
  - Jit: emit canonical body only. Refinement SpecIds get
    trap-stubs (or are simply not declared).
  - AotO2: emit canonical + every refinement.

- Annotation re-resolution pass between emission decision and
  CLIF lowering:
  - For each IR site with resolved_sid pointing at canonical,
    check whether an emitted refinement's narrowed_key covers
    this site's local Descrs. If yes, retarget resolved_sid to
    the refinement. If no, stay on canonical.
  - In Jit mode: skip the re-walk (everything stays on canonical).
  - In AotO2: re-walk produces direct-call annotations into
    refinements wherever the typer narrowing supports it.

- ABI per slot derived per resolved_sid via ArgRepr::from_descr.
  Cross-spec edge coercions inserted at call boundaries per
  (caller_local_repr, callee_expected_repr) mismatch.

- Golden CLIF split:
  - Existing fixtures/<name>/expected.clif renamed to
    expected.clif.aot.
  - New fixtures/<name>/expected.clif.jit generated.
  - tests/fixture_matrix.rs's golden_clif test loops over both,
    invoking the dump with EmitTarget::Jit vs ::AotO2 and
    comparing.
  - BLESS=1 regenerates both per fixture.

- `fz dump --emit clif` CLI grows a `--target {jit,aot}` flag
  (default: aot, matching today's behavior).

## What gets removed

- The implicit 'codegen always emits every reachable spec' path.
  Now governed by EmitTarget.
- The single expected.clif convention. Replaced by per-target
  goldens.

## Expected effect

- JIT goldens are small: ~1 CLIF function per fn in source +
  helper conts. fib_tailrec: ~5 functions. ast_eval_env: ~8.
- AOT goldens have refinements: fib_tailrec: ~8-10 functions
  (canonical + ~4 ABI-shifted refinements). ast_eval_env:
  similar.
- Direct-call performance recovered to or beyond pre-arc levels
  for AOT (narrow ABI calls, RawInt arithmetic loops).
- JIT compile time drops dramatically: one body per fn means
  Cranelift codegen-per-body cost is bounded by program size,
  not spec count. fixture_matrix wall: 60s → ~10-15s in debug.

## Acceptance

- All 166 fixture-matrix tests pass on both JIT and AOT paths
  with regenerated split goldens.
- JIT golden_clif vs AOT golden_clif diverge for fixtures with
  narrow-callable fns (fib_tailrec, list_primitives, etc.).
- Identical for fixtures with no refinement-eligible callsites
  (hello, simple closures, etc.).
- spec-quality diagnostic accurately reports refinement-emission
  status per target.

## See also

- fz-rfn.1 — canonical-first (blocking).
- fz-rfn.2 — refinement records (blocking).
- tests/fixture_matrix.rs:1177 — golden_clif test.
title: fz-rfn.3: per-target emission policy + annotation re-resolution + golden CLIF split
